name: Saffron Potato Onion Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the saffron threads in a separate bowl and pour hot water over them. Set aside for 30-40 minutes to steep.
2. Peel and cube 3 medium onions (approximately 3 cups). Add onions to a large saucepan with 2-3 tablespoons of water. Cover and sweat over low heat until transparent, stirring occasionally.
3. Do not let the onions burn.
4. Peel and cube 3 medium potatoes (approximately 4 cups), then add them along with the chicken broth (6 cups) to the saucepan. Bring to a simmer and cook for 25-30 minutes or until tender.
5. Puree the mixture in a food mill, blender, or by pressing through a fine mesh strainer. Return to the pan.
6. Stir in 1 cup of heavy cream along with the reserved saffron water. Heat before serving but do not boil.
7. Check seasoning and serve topped with additional whipped cream and thin slices of lemon. Serves 6.
